picks.narrative: The market is mispricing a clear starter advantage: Brandon Eisert (4.17 xFIP, with a 11.9% swinging-strike rate (above-average)) has a measurable edge over Andrew Painter (4.75 xFIP, 5.74 ERA — underperforming their process stats) — model prices CHW at 54.9% to win; Pinnacle implies 45.4% (+121) — a 9.5-point gap. A second angle confirms this: scheduling: today is a day game and the opponent played last night with 150 bullpen pitches over the last 3 days — a day-game-after-night-game setup with measurable roster fatigue. Model inputs solid, no critical gaps.

Eduardo Rodriguez (3.81 xFIP) has a quality edge over Zack Littell (5.61), yet the market has over-discounted WSH: model prices WSH at 47.4% to win; Pinnacle implies 40.7% (+144) — a 6.8-point gap. This comes with a caveat: arsenal matchup: the opposing lineup's handedness profile creates a modest pitch-type headwind for Zack Littell — factored in, but not enough to erase the overall edge. Key risk: Eduardo Rodriguez (3.81 xFIP) is the sharper starter — a 1.79-point gap that has to be overcome by WSH's other model signals. This pick requires those signals to hold.
